transcript_batch: Good evening. On the National Program of Talks tonight, we will be discussing content versus candy floss as the present progress in Indian writing in English. I'm Renuka Narayanan and we are very pleased to have with us Mr. Ravi Singh, a publisher of long standing and eminence, Mr. Chakravarti Mahajan who teaches cultural anthropology at Delhi University and Humra Qureshi, a writer and eminent columnist also of many years. Welcome to the show. We begin by setting out what seems to be the pattern in Indian writing in English from the 20th century. Earlier we had eminences like R. K. Narayan, Raja Rao, Mulk Raj Anand, in my view, niche writers. And they seemed to carry on the legacy of Premchand's writing in Hindi, which was that the sole task of fiction was to report misery, the misery of the rural classes, the urban poor. And this template of writing carried on until 19
